name: Monstera standleyana 'Aurea Variegated'
origin: SE Nicaragua to NW Colombia; variegated collector form in cultivation
aboutText: Monstera standleyana 'Aurea Variegated' is an attractive climbing aroid native from southern Nicaragua to northwestern Colombia. Highly sought after for its stable yellow-to-cream aurea variegation, the plant features glossy lanceolate leaves that rarely develop fenestrations. It climbs via short internodes and thrives under warm, humid, shaded understory conditions.

morphology.variegation: Yellow to cream aurea variegation in splashes and sectors
morphology.growthHabit: Climbing with aerial roots at nodes

fieldNotes.title: The Five-Color Shingle Hybrid
fieldNotes.author: Ariod Aaron
fieldNotes.content: Monstera standleyana (often sold incorrectly under the name Philodendron 'Cobra') is a climbing Monstera that differs from most of its relatives by rarely producing fenestrations. The 'Aurea Variegated' form features brilliant yellow-to-cream streaks and speckling. It ranges from Nicaragua to Colombia, growing in shaded understories. The thick, glossy texture of the lanceolate leaves reflects light to prevent burning under canopy gaps, and the aurea variegation remains stable throughout the life of the vine, climbing via short internodal roots.
